evidence: Product identification: Biaxially Oriented Polypropylene (BOPP) Film
Form: Flat flexible film
No Hazardous Substance(s) or Complex Substance(s) required for disclosure
The unused product is not specifically listed by the EPA as a hazardous waste nor is it formulated to contain materials which are listed as hazardous wastes.
No explicit mention of recycled, post-consumer, or post-industrial content

weight_evidence: No explicit weights provided in the SDS
quality_and_handling_evidence: Film should be stored avoiding high humidity and extreme temperature conditions.
Do not exceed an ambient temperature of 40°C and a relative humidity of 80%.
Protect the film rolls from sunlight direct exposure and do not place them nearby ovens, steam lines or other heat sources.
It is a good practice to remove the film from the store and transport it nearby the converting equipment at least 24 hrs before its use.
analysisData.recycling_relevance: The material can be re-used and recycled in accordance with current applicable laws and regulations.
evidence_statements: Product identification: Biaxially Oriented Polypropylene (BOPP) Film
Composition: Poly (propylene) (ca. 100%), Calcium carbonate, Titanium dioxide
Form: Flat flexible film
Odor/Color: None / White
Relative density (g/cm3): 0.55 – 0.96
Softening point ca.165 (°C)
The material can be re-used and recycled
No hazardous substances disclosed
limitations: No batch-specific physical evidence such as photographs, actual shipment form, contamination, or storage condition.
SDS does not specify if film is strictly virgin or could potentially be produced with recycled content in some grades.
No explicit indication of contamination, packaging, or color beyond generic 'white'.
No explicit processing type stated; inferred from product form and description.
unknowns: Actual appearance and condition of specific shipment or batch.
Presence or absence of contamination or non-white coloration in a specific lot.

Whether photographic or other evidence could override/document visible nonconformance.

json.text:
SAFETY DATA SHEETS
according to
Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labeling of Chemicals
29 CFR 1910.1200
Code: SDS-DP-002 Effective date: 01.15.2021
Version number: 04 
Reviewed on: 01.05.2021
SECTION 1 PRODUCT MANUFACTURER / IDENTIFICATION.
1.1 Product identification: Biaxially Oriented Polypropylene (BOPP) Film
1.2 Trade Name: White Film (See Section 16 for applicable grades)
1.3 Intended use: Packaging • Printing • Labeling • Lamination
Restrictions on use: Stick to the intended use of the product
1.4 Manufacturer: 
INNOVIA FILMS ZACAPU SA DE CV
Av. Colorines N° 255 Col. Centro • 58600 • Zacapu • Michoacán • México
1.5 Emergency phone number: 24 Hour Health Emergency: (800) 726-2015
Supplier General Contact: (011) 52+436 3638000
SECTION 2 HAZARD IDENTIFICATION
2.1 Classification of the substance: This product is not classified as dangerous (See section 15).
2.2 Label elements, including precautionary
statements:
- Pictograms: n.a.
- Signal word: n.a.
- Hazard statement: n.a.
- Precautionary statements: P261 Avoid breathing fumes / vapors.
P280 Wear protective gloves/protective clothing/eye protection/face protection.
2.3 Hazards which do not result in 
No specific danger is associated to the normal industrial utilization of the
classification: product. However, it is to be considered that plastic film left of the ground of
working areas are slippery, and that during converting of film rolls electrostatic
discharges can be generated.
SECTION 3 COMPOSITION
3.1 Chemical identity: Poly (propylene) (ca. 100%)
*Calcium carbonate
Titanium dioxide
Form: 
Flat flexible film
Odor/Color: None / White
3.2 CAS-N°: 
9003-07-0
471-34-1
13463-67-7
3.3 No Hazardous Substance(s) or Complex Substance(s) required for disclosure.
SECTION 4 FIRST-AID MEASURES
4.1 Inhalation/Ingestion: Considering the physical form of the product, inhalation and/or ingestion are not
likely to be possible during normal use.
P304•P340. In case of the inhalation of fumes, the affected person should be
removed to fresh air as soon as possible, kept warm and artificial respiration
applied as necessary.
Seek medical assistance.
Skin contact: P302•P320. Skin or clothing contaminated by molten polymer should be
1/4

4.2 Special treatment needed:
drenched with clean cold water until cool. In case of skin contact by molten
material, the patient should be referred for immediate medical attention and
under no circumstances should any attempt be made to peel the solidified
polymer off the skin.
n.a.
SECTION 5 FIRE FIGHTING MEASURES
General Recommendations: Polypropylene film will burn if exposed to flames, giving off harmful fumes that
should not be inhaled. Molten droplets of polymer can be produced, which could
ignite adjacent flammable and/or combustible materials.
Self-ignition temperature:
> 400 °C
Combustion products:
By direct ignition the product burns with flames, developing H2O, CO2 and, in
case of oxygen defect, also CO. The fire products are irritant and toxic.
Decomposition products:
In the growth stages of fires (particularly between 400 and 700°C) other
products as hydrocarbons and aldehydes (acetaldehyde, crotonaldehyde)
may be formed.
5.1 Recommended extinguishing media Water Fog / Water mist • Carbon Dioxide • Not recommended: Water Jets as
initial extinguishing media.
5.2 Specific Hazards: 
None.
5.3 Special protective actions: 
Firefighters should use standard protective equipment. Wear suitable self-
contained breathing equipment in case of risk of exposure to vapors or fumes.
SECTION 6 ACCIDENTAL RELEASE MEASURES
Keep away from heat or flame. Collect the film in order to avoid possible slipping of personnel on the floor.
SECTION 7 HANDLING AND STORAGE
7.1 Precautions for safe handling:
7.2 Conditions for safe storage:
Unwinding, winding and passage of the film through nips and cover rollers will
tend to generate a strong electrostatic charge of the web. Static discharge
devices should be properly positioned as such points to eliminate the charge and
to prevent uncontrolled discharge from the web. This is particularly required in
hazardous atmospheres and to protect personnel handling the film.
The product does not present special safety requirements during handling and
storage; however, in order to maintain their properties and to ensure a good
processability, the film should be stored avoiding high humidity and extreme
temperature conditions. It is especially recommended to avoid any moisture
condensation.
Do not exceed an ambient temperature of 40°C and a relative humidity of 80%;
protect the film rolls from sunlight direct exposure and do not place them nearby
ovens, steam lines or other heat sources. It is also a good practice to remove the
film from the store and transport it nearby the converting equipment at least 24
hrs before its use.
SECTION 8 PERSONAL PROTECTION
i. During normal handling protective gloves and the usual equipment are
recommended.
ii. If there is any possibility of having contact with molten film (e.g.:
nearby the sealing bars or the heated rollers of packaging machines),
heat protective gloves are recommended.
2/4

SECTION 9 PHYSICAL AND CHEMICAL PROPERTIES
not apply
i. Appearance: Film xi. Vapour pressure:
ii. Odor: none xii. Vapour density: not apply
iii. Odor threshold: not apply xiii. 
Relative density (g/cm
3
): 
0.55 – 0.96
iv. pH-value (g/H2O): not apply xiv. Solubility: Insoluble
v. Softening point ca.165 xv. Partition coefficient: n- not apply
(°C): octane/water
vi. Boiling point (°C): not apply xvi. Auto-ignition temperature >400
(°C):
vii. Flash point (°C): 350 xvii. Decomposition not apply
temperature (°C):
viii. Evaporation rate: not apply xviii. Viscosity: not apply
ix. Flammability: not apply
x. Explosive limits: not apply
SECTION 10 STABILITY AND REACTIVITY
10.1 Reactivity: Non reactive 10.4 Conditions to avoid: Do not heat to temperature exceeding
235°C
10.2 Chemical stability: Stable at normal 10.5 Incompatible Unknown
handling materials:
10.3 Possibility of hazardous None 10.6 Hazardous H2O, CO2 and CO.
reactions: decomposition products:
SECTION 11 TOXICOLOGICAL INFORMATION
Not toxic under normal conditions of handling and use.
SECTION 12 ECOLOGICAL INFOMATION
When disposed of under orderly conditions no environmental damage effects occur. When burning under free air conditions only
CO2, CO and water arise. Does not contain additives based on heavy metals like cadmium (Cd), lead (Pb), mercury (Hg) or
chromium
VI 
(Cr
VI
).
Biodegradation –Expected to be persistent
Bioaccumulation –n.a
SECTION 13 DISPOSAL CONSIDERATIONS
The material can be re-used and recycled in accordance with current applicable laws and regulations. Disposal must be done
according to all regulations.
The unused product is not specifically listed by the EPA as a hazardous waste nor is it formulated to contain materials which
are listed as hazardous wastes.
SECTION 14 TRANSPORT INFORMATION
IMDG
RID/ADR Not classified as dangerous goods.
GGVE/GGVS
ICAO/IATA-DGR
SECTION 15 REGULATORY INFORMATION
The product is not subject to the German Gefahrstoff – Verordnung (Ge StoffV).
If FDA Compliance Certificate is required, please contact Customer Service.
3/4

SECTION 16 OTHER INFORMATION
Applicable grades: ELR • ERH • EUP • LLR • LOD • LOH • LQH • LRH • LVH • LWR • LWH • LWP • PRH • QAD • QHH • QID •
QJD • QLD • QNH • QPD • VLD • WNH • WOPP • Development products.
Statements are made due to latest state of our knowledge. They shall describe our product with regard to safety requirements,
thus do not mean a guarantee for certain characteristics. It is the user's responsibility to satisfy itself that the product is suitable
for the intended use. Alteration of this document is strictly prohibited, except to the extent required by law, re-publication or
retransmission of this document, in whole or in part, is not permitted. This version invalidates the previous one.
4/4

MATERIAL SAFETY DATA SHEET

NOTE: The Hazard Communication Standard (HCS) requires chemical manufacturers, distributors, or importers to provide Safety Data

11. TOXICOLOGICAL INFORMATION
CANCER INFORMATION:
Not listed as a carcinogen by NTP (National Toxicology Program); not regulated as a carcinogen by OSHA
(Occupational Safety & Health Administration).
REPORTED HUMAN EFFECTS:
Over the past 40-plus years, Ti received only sporadic reports of adverse effects from workers handling this
product. They consisted of an occasional sore throat or headache developed while thermoforming or handling
heated films and undocumented reports of rashes on the hands, face, or arms. Tests on human skin have shown
that polypropylene does not cause chemical irritation to the skin and is not a skin sensitizing (allergenic) material.
REPORTED ANIMAL EFFECTS:

MATERIAL SAFETY DATA SHEET
MSDS No.: MSDS 01-17 Date: 21 March 2024 PAGE: 4 of 5
Polypropylene has been demonstrated to be practically non-toxic in studies carried out in animals and humans in
many laboratories. Its acute oral LD50 for rats is greater than 5,000 mg/kg body weight, the maximum feasible
dose. Polypropylene is not absorbed through the skin and thus does not present a systemic hazard in handling.
12. ECOLOGICAL INFORMATION
This product is not soluble in water. Apply good ecological practices in the disposal of this product.
13. DISPOSAL CONSIDERATION
WASTE DISPOSAL METHOD:
Landfilling in a licensed facility in accordance with local, state, and federal industrial waste regulations is the
recommended disposal method. Incineration in a licensed facility is a suitable alternative. Mixing the waste with
wood or paper wastes aids in assuring complete combustion.
Refer to Section 15 for specific Federal Environmental and Regulatory Data regarding use or disposal of this
product.
14. TRANSPORT INFORMATION
This product is not classified as a hazardous material for transportation according to the DOT, ADR/RID, IMDG,
IATA.

SECTION 5 FIRE FIGHTING MEASURES
General Recommendations: Polypropylene film will burn if exposed to flames, giving off harmful fumes that
should not be inhaled. Molten droplets of polymer can be produced, which could
ignite adjacent flammable and/or combustible materials.
Self-ignition temperature:
> 400 °C
Combustion products:
By direct ignition the product burns with flames, developing H2O, CO2 and, in
case of oxygen defect, also CO. The fire products are irritant and toxic.
Decomposition products:
In the growth stages of fires (particularly between 400 and 700°C) other
products as hydrocarbons and aldehydes (acetaldehyde, crotonaldehyde)
may be formed.
5.1 Recommended extinguishing media Water Fog / Water mist • Carbon Dioxide • Not recommended: Water Jets as
initial extinguishing media.
5.2 Specific Hazards: 
None.
5.3 Special protective actions: 
Firefighters should use standard protective equipment. Wear suitable self-
contained breathing equipment in case of risk of exposure to vapors or fumes.
SECTION 6 ACCIDENTAL RELEASE MEASURES
Keep away from heat or flame. Collect the film in order to avoid possible slipping of personnel on the floor.
SECTION 7 HANDLING AND STORAGE
7.1 Precautions for safe handling:
7.2 Conditions for safe storage:
Unwinding, winding and passage of the film through nips and cover rollers will
tend to generate a strong electrostatic charge of the web. Static discharge
devices should be properly positioned as such points to eliminate the charge and
to prevent uncontrolled discharge from the web. This is particularly required in
hazardous atmospheres and to protect personnel handling the film.
The product does not present special safety requirements during handling and
storage; however, in order to maintain their properties and to ensure a good
processability, the film should be stored avoiding high humidity and extreme
temperature conditions. It is especially recommended to avoid any moisture
condensation.
Do not exceed an ambient temperature of 40°C and a relative humidity of 80%;
protect the film rolls from sunlight direct exposure and do not place them nearby
ovens, steam lines or other heat sources. It is also a good practice to remove the
film from the store and transport it nearby the converting equipment at least 24
hrs before its use.
SECTION 8 PERSONAL PROTECTION
i. During normal handling protective gloves and the usual equipment are
recommended.
ii. If there is any possibility of having contact with molten film (e.g.:
nearby the sealing bars or the heated rollers of packaging machines),
heat protective gloves are recommended.

• UNUSUAL FIRE AND EXPLOSION HAZARDS: None
• INCOMPATIBILITY: Avoid contact with hot or concentrated nitric and perchloric acids and fuming sulfuric acid at
60
o
C (140
o
F) or above.
• HAZARDOUS DECOMPOSITION PRODUCTS: None
• HAZARDOUS PRODUCTS OF COMBUSTION: Combustion products depend on other materials present in
the fire and on fire conditions. In most cases, these will be carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide. Usually,
carbon dioxide will be the principal combustion product. In some cases, irritating partial combustion products
will be formed.
• HAZARDOUS POLYMERIZATION: Will not occur.
11. TOXICOLOGICAL INFORMATION
CANCER INFORMATION:
Not listed as a carcinogen by NTP (National Toxicology Program); not regulated as a carcinogen by OSHA
(Occupational Safety & Health Administration).
REPORTED HUMAN EFFECTS:
Over the past 40-plus years, Ti received only sporadic reports of adverse effects from workers handling this
product. They consisted of an occasional sore throat or headache developed while thermoforming or handling
heated films and undocumented reports of rashes on the hands, face, or arms. Tests on human skin have shown
that polypropylene does not cause chemical irritation to the skin and is not a skin sensitizing (allergenic) material.
REPORTED ANIMAL EFFECTS:

MATERIAL SAFETY DATA SHEET
MSDS No.: MSDS 01-17 Date: 21 March 2024 PAGE: 4 of 5
Polypropylene has been demonstrated to be practically non-toxic in studies carried out in animals and humans in
many laboratories. Its acute oral LD50 for rats is greater than 5,000 mg/kg body weight, the maximum feasible
dose. Polypropylene is not absorbed through the skin and thus does not present a systemic hazard in handling.
12. ECOLOGICAL INFORMATION
This product is not soluble in water. Apply good ecological practices in the disposal of this product.
13. DISPOSAL CONSIDERATION
WASTE DISPOSAL METHOD:
Landfilling in a licensed facility in accordance with local, state, and federal industrial waste regulations is the
recommended disposal method. Incineration in a licensed facility is a suitable alternative. Mixing the waste with
wood or paper wastes aids in assuring complete combustion.
Refer to Section 15 for specific Federal Environmental and Regulatory Data regarding use or disposal of this
product.
14. TRANSPORT INFORMATION
This product is not classified as a hazardous material for transportation according to the DOT, ADR/RID, IMDG,
IATA.
15. REGULATIONS
The following environmental and regulatory data are provided to assist users of this product in defining their
regulatory environmental compliance obligations.

C. CERCLA: (40 CFR 302.4 HAZARDOUS SUBSTANCE & REPORTABLE QUANTITIES)
This product does NOT contain any hazardous substances listed in 40 CFR 302.4.
D. RCRA INFORMATION:
This product is not listed in federal hazardous waste regulation 40 CFR 261.33, paragraph (e) or (f) - i.e.,
chemical products that are considered hazardous if they become wastes. It does not exhibit any of the
hazardous characteristics listed in 40 CFR 261, Subpart C. State or local hazardous waste regulations may
apply if they are different from the federal regulation.
E. OTHER: None.

MATERIAL SAFETY DATA SHEET
MSDS No.: MSDS 01-17 Date: 21 March 2024 PAGE: 5 of 5

SAFETY DATA SHEETS
according to
Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labeling of Chemicals
29 CFR 1910.1200
Code: SDS-DP-002 Effective date: 01.15.2021
Version number: 04 
Reviewed on: 01.05.2021
SECTION 1 PRODUCT MANUFACTURER / IDENTIFICATION.
1.1 Product identification: Biaxially Oriented Polypropylene (BOPP) Film
1.2 Trade Name: White Film (See Section 16 for applicable grades)
1.3 Intended use: Packaging • Printing • Labeling • Lamination
Restrictions on use: Stick to the intended use of the product
1.4 Manufacturer: 
INNOVIA FILMS ZACAPU SA DE CV
Av. Colorines N° 255 Col. Centro • 58600 • Zacapu • Michoacán • México
1.5 Emergency phone number: 24 Hour Health Emergency: (800) 726-2015
Supplier General Contact: (011) 52+436 3638000
SECTION 2 HAZARD IDENTIFICATION
2.1 Classification of the substance: This product is not classified as dangerous (See section 15).
2.2 Label elements, including precautionary
statements:
- Pictograms: n.a.
- Signal word: n.a.
- Hazard statement: n.a.
- Precautionary statements: P261 Avoid breathing fumes / vapors.
P280 Wear protective gloves/protective clothing/eye protection/face protection.
2.3 Hazards which do not result in 
No specific danger is associated to the normal industrial utilization of the
classification: product. However, it is to be considered that plastic film left of the ground of
working areas are slippery, and that during converting of film rolls electrostatic
discharges can be generated.
SECTION 3 COMPOSITION
3.1 Chemical identity: Poly (propylene) (ca. 100%)
*Calcium carbonate
Titanium dioxide
Form: 
Flat flexible film
Odor/Color: None / White
3.2 CAS-N°: 
9003-07-0
471-34-1
13463-67-7
3.3 No Hazardous Substance(s) or Complex Substance(s) required for disclosure.
SECTION 4 FIRST-AID MEASURES
4.1 Inhalation/Ingestion: Considering the physical form of the product, inhalation and/or ingestion are not
likely to be possible during normal use.
P304•P340. In case of the inhalation of fumes, the affected person should be
removed to fresh air as soon as possible, kept warm and artificial respiration
applied as necessary.
Seek medical assistance.
Skin contact: P302•P320. Skin or clothing contaminated by molten polymer should be
1/4

4.2 Special treatment needed:
drenched with clean cold water until cool. In case of skin contact by molten
material, the patient should be referred for immediate medical attention and
under no circumstances should any attempt be made to peel the solidified
polymer off the skin.
n.a.
SECTION 5 FIRE FIGHTING MEASURES
General Recommendations: Polypropylene film will burn if exposed to flames, giving off harmful fumes that
should not be inhaled. Molten droplets of polymer can be produced, which could
ignite adjacent flammable and/or combustible materials.
Self-ignition temperature:
> 400 °C
Combustion products:
By direct ignition the product burns with flames, developing H2O, CO2 and, in
case of oxygen defect, also CO. The fire products are irritant and toxic.
Decomposition products:
In the growth stages of fires (particularly between 400 and 700°C) other
products as hydrocarbons and aldehydes (acetaldehyde, crotonaldehyde)
may be formed.
5.1 Recommended extinguishing media Water Fog / Water mist • Carbon Dioxide • Not recommended: Water Jets as
initial extinguishing media.
5.2 Specific Hazards: 
None.
5.3 Special protective actions: 
Firefighters should use standard protective equipment. Wear suitable self-
contained breathing equipment in case of risk of exposure to vapors or fumes.
SECTION 6 ACCIDENTAL RELEASE MEASURES
Keep away from heat or flame. Collect the film in order to avoid possible slipping of personnel on the floor.
SECTION 7 HANDLING AND STORAGE
7.1 Precautions for safe handling:
7.2 Conditions for safe storage:
Unwinding, winding and passage of the film through nips and cover rollers will
tend to generate a strong electrostatic charge of the web. Static discharge
devices should be properly positioned as such points to eliminate the charge and
to prevent uncontrolled discharge from the web. This is particularly required in
hazardous atmospheres and to protect personnel handling the film.
The product does not present special safety requirements during handling and
storage; however, in order to maintain their properties and to ensure a good
processability, the film should be stored avoiding high humidity and extreme
temperature conditions. It is especially recommended to avoid any moisture
condensation.
Do not exceed an ambient temperature of 40°C and a relative humidity of 80%;
protect the film rolls from sunlight direct exposure and do not place them nearby
ovens, steam lines or other heat sources. It is also a good practice to remove the
film from the store and transport it nearby the converting equipment at least 24
hrs before its use.
SECTION 8 PERSONAL PROTECTION
i. During normal handling protective gloves and the usual equipment are
recommended.
ii. If there is any possibility of having contact with molten film (e.g.:
nearby the sealing bars or the heated rollers of packaging machines),
heat protective gloves are recommended.

HAZARDOUS POLYMERIZATION: Will not occur.
6. ACCIDENTAL RELEASE MEASURES
SPILL PROCEDURES:
If material is not contaminated, place into clean containers for use.
If contaminated, place into containers for disposal.
7. HANDLING & STORAGE
• APPROPRIATE HYGIENIC PRACTICES: Wash thoroughly after handling, and before eating, drinking, or
smoking.
• PERSONAL PROTECTIVE EQUIPMENT: Safety glasses
• WORK PRACTICES: Eyewash fountains and safety showers should be easily accessible.
• HANDLING AND STORAGE PRECAUTIONS: Store in a sprinklered warehouse. Keep temperatures below
32 
o
C (90
o
F) for quality control. This product may react with hot or concentrated nitric and perchloric acids,
fuming sulfuric acid at 60
o
C (140
o
F) or above, and should not be stored near such materials.
• ENGINEERING CONTROLS: Provide adequate ventilation.
• PROTECTIVE MEASURES DURING REPAIR AND MAINTENANCE: Keep area clean. Product will
melt/burn.
8. EXPOSURE CONTROLS & PERSONAL PROTECTION
• SIGNS AND SYMPTOMS OF OVEREXPOSURE IN THE WORKPLACE: There are no known or expected
signs or symptoms of overexposure in the workplace under normal conditions of use.
• EMERGENCY & FIRST AID PROCEDURES: None necessary.
• MEDICAL CONDITIONS GENERALLY RECOGNIZED AS BEING AGGRAVATED BY EXPOSURE: None
known.
• PRIMARY ROUTES OF EXPOSURE/ENTRY: Skin contact (absorption not expected).
• The possibility of hazardous fumes being generated during heat-sealing procedures has been considered, but
the temperatures employed in these operations 120-150
o
C (250-300
o
F) are insufficient to cause
decomposition. Therefore, exposure to decomposition products does not occur under normal conditions of
use.
• Because the possibility of inadvertent overheating exists, potential health hazards associated with repeated

MATERIAL SAFETY DATA SHEET
MSDS No.: MSDS 01-17 Date: 21 March 2024 PAGE: 3 of 5
exposure to polypropylene decomposition products at various temperatures has been investigated. In these
studies, no adverse effects were observed among rats exposed 6 hr/day, 5 day/wk for a total of 10 exposures
to the thermal decomposition products of polypropylene formed from heating the plastic in air to 525
o
F or
575
o
F (polypropylene extruder temperatures). From these data, it is concluded that fumes generated at
temperatures at or below 300
o
C (575
o
F) are not hazardous.
9. PHYSICAL & CHEMICAL PROPERTIES
• BOILING POINT: N/A
• VAPOR PRESSURE AT 20 
o 
C: N/A
• VAPOR DENSITY: N/A
• VOLATILE (WT.), %: < 0.4
• MELTING POINT: Above 160
o
C (320
o
F)
• FLASH POINT: Above 329
o
C (625
o
F) Setchkin
• FLAMMABLE LIMITS: N/A
• AUTOIGNITION TEMPERATURE: Above 35
o
C (675
o
F) Setchkin
• SOLUBILITY IN WATER: Negligible
• SPECIFIC GRAVITY: 0.65 - 0.92
• pH: N/A
• EVAPORATION RATE: N/A
10. STABILITY & REACTIVITY
• STABILITY CONSIDERATIONS: Stable.
• REACTIVITY: Not reactive.
• UNUSUAL FIRE AND EXPLOSION HAZARDS: None
• INCOMPATIBILITY: Avoid contact with hot or concentrated nitric and perchloric acids and fuming sulfuric acid at
60
o
C (140
o
F) or above.
• HAZARDOUS DECOMPOSITION PRODUCTS: None
• HAZARDOUS PRODUCTS OF COMBUSTION: Combustion products depend on other materials present in
the fire and on fire conditions. In most cases, these will be carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide. Usually,
carbon dioxide will be the principal combustion product. In some cases, irritating partial combustion products
will be formed.
• HAZARDOUS POLYMERIZATION: Will not occur.
11. TOXICOLOGICAL INFORMATION
CANCER INFORMATION:
Not listed as a carcinogen by NTP (National Toxicology Program); not regulated as a carcinogen by OSHA
(Occupational Safety & Health Administration).
REPORTED HUMAN EFFECTS:
Over the past 40-plus years, Ti received only sporadic reports of adverse effects from workers handling this
product. They consisted of an occasional sore throat or headache developed while thermoforming or handling
heated films and undocumented reports of rashes on the hands, face, or arms. Tests on human skin have shown
that polypropylene does not cause chemical irritation to the skin and is not a skin sensitizing (allergenic) material.
REPORTED ANIMAL EFFECTS:

MATERIAL SAFETY DATA SHEET
MSDS No.: MSDS 01-17 Date: 21 March 2024 PAGE: 4 of 5
Polypropylene has been demonstrated to be practically non-toxic in studies carried out in animals and humans in
many laboratories. Its acute oral LD50 for rats is greater than 5,000 mg/kg body weight, the maximum feasible
dose. Polypropylene is not absorbed through the skin and thus does not present a systemic hazard in handling.
12. ECOLOGICAL INFORMATION
This product is not soluble in water. Apply good ecological practices in the disposal of this product.
13. DISPOSAL CONSIDERATION
WASTE DISPOSAL METHOD:
Landfilling in a licensed facility in accordance with local, state, and federal industrial waste regulations is the
recommended disposal method. Incineration in a licensed facility is a suitable alternative. Mixing the waste with
wood or paper wastes aids in assuring complete combustion.
Refer to Section 15 for specific Federal Environmental and Regulatory Data regarding use or disposal of this
product.
14. TRANSPORT INFORMATION
This product is not classified as a hazardous material for transportation according to the DOT, ADR/RID, IMDG,
IATA.
15. REGULATIONS
The following environmental and regulatory data are provided to assist users of this product in defining their
regulatory environmental compliance obligations.
A. PRODUCT COMPOSITION:
PRODUCT (P) or TRADE NAME or
COMPONENT NO. CHEMICAL COMPONENT CAS NUMBER WT. PERCENT
P Polypropylene Homopolymer and Copolymer (proprietary mixture) 100%
films with no post-consumer recycled content
B. SARA TITLE III: (See footnotes)
PRODUCT (P) or SEC. 304 SEC. 302
COMPONENT EHS EHS SEC. 311/312 SEC. 313
NO. RQ (lbs) TPQ (lbs) HAZARD CATEGORY TOXIC CHEMICAL (YES, NO)
P N/A N/A NHH, NPH NO
C. CERCLA: (40 CFR 302.4 HAZARDOUS SUBSTANCE & REPORTABLE QUANTITIES)
This product does NOT contain any hazardous substances listed in 40 CFR 302.4.
D. RCRA INFORMATION:
This product is not listed in federal hazardous waste regulation 40 CFR 261.33, paragraph (e) or (f) - i.e.,
chemical products that are considered hazardous if they become wastes. It does not exhibit any of the
hazardous characteristics listed in 40 CFR 261, Subpart C. State or local hazardous waste regulations may
apply if they are different from the federal regulation.
E. OTHER: None.

MATERIAL SAFETY DATA SHEET
MSDS No.: MSDS 01-17 Date: 21 March 2024 PAGE: 5 of 5
FOOTNOTES:
SEC. 302 - Threshold Planning Quantity, Extremely Hazardous Substance (EHS) (40 CFR 355 Emergency Planning and
Notification regulations)
N/A: This chemical is not an EHS. Therefore, there is no Threshold Planning Quantity (TPQ).
SEC. 304 - Reportable Quantity for Releases of an EHS (40 CFR 355, Appendix A)
N/A: This chemical is not an EHS. Therefore, there is no Reportable Quantity (RQ).
SEC. 311/312 - 40 CFR 370 Hazardous Chemical Reporting Requirements
"Hazard Categories" HC-1 Immediate (acute) health hazard; HC-2 Delayed (chronic) health hazard; HC-3 Fire hazard; HC-4
Sudden release of pressure hazard; HC-5 Reactive hazard; NHH Not a health hazard; NPH Not a physical hazard.
SEC. 313 - 40 CFR 372 Toxic Chemical Release Reporting Requirements
NO: This component is NOT subject to the reporting requirements of Section 313 of Title III of the Superfund Amendments
and Reauthorization Act of 1986 and 40 CFR Part 372 Toxic Chemical Reporting requirements.
YES: This component is subject to the reporting requirements of Section 313 of Title III of the Superfund Amendments and
Reauthorization Act of 1986 and 40 CFR Part 372 Toxic Chemical Reporting requirements. Percent composition (or estimated
range) is listed above.
N/A: This product is a mixture. As such, it is not listed as a Toxic Chemical under 40 CFR 372, Sect. 313 reporting
requirements. Reportable constituents are listed individually where they exceed threshold concentration limits.
16. OTHER INFORMATION
Explanation of acronyms:
HMIS: Hazardous Materials Identification System rating for product as supplied.
CASRN: Chemical Abstract Services Registry Number
OSHA: Occupational Safety & Health Administration
EEC /EU /CE: European Economic Community / European Union / Council European (European Council)
CFR: Code of Federal Regulations
DOT: Department of Transportation
IATA: International Air Transport Association
IMDG: International Maritime Dangerous Goods
RID: International Rule for Transport of Dangerous Substances by Railway
ADR: Accord Européen relatif au transport international des marchandises dangereuses par route,
also known as the European Agreement concerning the International Carriage of Dangerous
Goods by Road.
AIHA WEEL: American Industrial Hygienists Association – Workplace Environmental Exposure Level.
OSHA: Occupational Safety and Health Administration.
TLV: Registered trademark of American Conference of Governmental Industrial Hygienists for
Threshold Limit Values.
TWA: Time Weighted Average
STEL: Short term exposure limit (See 29 CFR 1910.1048, March 1, 1989, revision)
C: Ceiling exposure concentration (See 29 CFR 1910.1000, March 1, 1989, rev.)
SKIN: May be absorbed through skin (See 29 CFR 1910.1048, March 1, 1989, rev.)
N/A: Not applicable
------------------------
THE INFORMATION AND RECOMMENDATIONS CONTAINED IN THIS MATERIAL SAFETY DATA SHEET WERE COMPILED
FROM SOURCES BELIEVED TO BE RELIABLE AND TO REPRESENT THE MOST REASONABLE CURRENT OPINION ON THE
SUBJECT WHEN THE MSDS WAS PREPARED. NO WARRANTY, GUARANTY OR REPRESENTATION IS MADE AS TO THE
CORRECTNESS OR SUFFICIENCY OF THE INFORMATION. THE USER OF THIS PRODUCT MUST DECIDE WHAT SAFETY
MEASURES ARE NECESSARY TO SAFELY USE THIS PRODUCT, EITHER ALONE OR IN COMBINATION WITH OTHER
PRODUCTS, AND DETERMINE ITS ENVIRONMENTAL REGULATORY COMPLIANCE OBLIGATIONS UNDER ANY APPLICABLE
FEDERAL OR STATE LAWS.

It is a good practice to remove the film from the store and transport it nearby the converting equipment at least 24 hrs before its use.

unknowns: Actual appearance and condition of specific shipment or batch.

evidence: Sheets resemble typical plastic or filmic release liner, not composite or paper-only.

corrections.reason: No gross debris, dirt, or foreign object is visible—the observed 'contaminant risk' is intrinsic (printing/labels/adhesive), not foreign matter.
evidence: Close-up inspection reveals only intended print and potential adhesive, no visible soiling or debris.

confirmed_observations: visible_quality_risks includes paper labels and print/ink
unsupported_agent_1_claims: possible_polymer: Unclear (partial disagreement due to context and visual clues, but not fully supportable as BOPP strictly from image)

evidence: Visible open Gaylord with loose, flat, printed sheets; estimated >2/3 full; no visible cores or rolls.
Numerous printed text/graphic blocks typical of labelstocks or release liner scrap; each sheet has label area and presumed adhesive residue or paper
Webform, Agent 1 and direct image show mixed print
commercial_impact: High risk for adhesive/ink contamination in resin recycling streams; reduces suitability for high-purity film/feedstock applications

evidence: All exposed scrap area includes colored and black print
commercial_impact: Ink removal would be required for clean PCR reprocessing

evidence_statements: The image shows an open Gaylord containing loose, flexible, flat sheets with multicolor printing and legible text blocks consistent with trim scrap from a printer's label operation.
There are no visible paperboard or plastic cores; the sheets are laid in Gaylord with no roll compaction.
Sheets are opaque, visually mostly white with prominent yellow and black printing; print area is extensive on most sheets.
No gross soiling, water damage, or solid debris is present.
The Gaylord is stretch wrapped, placed on a pallet, and located indoors (facility context visible at edge).
Contaminant risk is intrinsic to the printing/label/adhesive nature rather than foreign object contamination.
limitations: Polymer, liner type, and precise adhesive/ink chemistry cannot be confirmed from the image alone—visual impression supports filmic release liner, consistent with but not confirmatory for BOPP.
Unit count is limited to the single visible Gaylord; total site inventory is not shown.
Weight per Gaylord is not visually estimable due to lack of visible scale, labels, or known packing density.
unknowns: Polymer family (BOPP or other film only inferred from webform context and not confirmed visually)
Precise mix of adhesive, paper, and plastic (chemical analysis required)
Print ink type and potential for downstream ink/adhesive incompatibility
Any potential hidden debris or contaminant below visible top layer

messages:
System: # SYSTEM - Agent 2 Multimodal Lead Adjudicator V5.5

You are Agent 2 for Scrap Management Inc. Your direct-image phase has independently inspected every authoritative original image immediately upstream. You now reconcile those image audits with the complete Cognito webform, every Agent 1 Visual Material Evidence Packet, and every PDF Material Evidence Report.

Return supported business conclusions and explicitly preserve conflicts, evidence, assumptions, limitations, and unknowns. Never silently copy Agent 1. Correct it when the direct image audits disagree.

SOURCE AUTHORITY BY FIELD
1. Seller form: seller-stated quantity, cadence language, company, location, stated source, and stated contamination.
2. Current original images: current material form, containment, support unit, visible color, visible contamination, storage condition, and visible inventory units.
3. PDFs: polymer, composition, specifications, technical properties, explicit weights, and explicit process statements.
4. Direct Agent 2 audits outrank Agent 1 for visual facts.
5. A failed image or PDF analysis is not contrary evidence. Preserve it for manual review.

NORMALIZATION INVARIANTS
- Process Type values: Injection Molding; Blow Molding; Extrusion - Film; Extrusion - Sheet; Extrusion - Pipe/Profile; Thermoforming; Compression Molding; Rotational Molding; Compounding/Pelletizing; Foam Processing; Other; Unclear.
- Material Type values: Post Industrial; Post Commercial; Post Consumer; Virgin; Repro; Unclear.
- Form is the recyclable material form. Packaging is containment/support for shipment.
- Loose film in Gaylords => material_form Loose, packaging Gaylords.
- Film rolls on pallets => material_form Film Rolls, packaging Pallets.
- Baled film => material_form Film Bales, packaging Bales.
- Never infer form solely from packaging.
- Mixed is a valid commercial color. Printed multicolor recyclable material is Mixed. Ignore pallet, container, floor, equipment, and background colors.
- Do not infer polymer solely from appearance.

QUANTITY AND CADENCE
- Distinguish current inventory, pounds per load, and recurring shipment cadence.
- "3 loads" is a current load count unless time-basis language establishes cadence. It is never 3 pounds.
- Extract explicit weekly, monthly, every-other-week, twice-monthly, quarterly, annual, and range language.
- Do not perform weekly/monthly conversion arithmetic. Deterministic code owns conversion.
- Unknown cadence is null, not zero. Use zero only when the seller explicitly states zero.
- Ongoing but unspecified supply has null cadence and a clarification request.

WEIGHT ESTIMATION
- Deduplicate visible units across images using candidate duplicate groups, spatial identity, labels, damage marks, roll/core geometry, and scene overlap.
- Emit unique units and per-unit gross weight ranges only when supportable.
- Do not deduct tare or total the lot. Deterministic code owns arithmetic.
- Mark whether the estimate covers the complete inventory or visible inventory only.
- Outdoor/moisture conditions widen uncertainty. Do not estimate exact water content.

QUALITY RISKS
- Outdoor-stored baled film => Moisture, High, Operationally Assumed.
- Preserve commercial routing impacts without inventing measurements.

QUALIFICATION
Preserve the existing thresholds:
- Hot: confirmed >=10,000 lb or equivalent full truckload, plastic, business/commercial/industrial source.
- Cold: explicit residential/individual, <8,000 lb, drop-off, non-plastic, or disallowed material.
- Unclear Hot: potentially qualified but missing critical confirmation. Unclear Hot routes to HOT.

Return raw structured JSON only through the attached parser. Every top-level key must exist.

OUTPUT CONTRACT HARD RULES
- decision must be exactly one of: Hot, Unclear Hot, Cold.
- airtable_target must be exactly HOT or COLD. Never return a table name, lead label, routing description, or offer ID in this field.
- email_template must be exactly one template identifier: Qualified A, Rejection B, Rejection C, Rejection D, or Rejection E. Never draft the email body in this field.
- Nullable numeric fields are genuinely nullable. When no defensible gross unit weight exists, set gross_weight_low_lbs_each and gross_weight_high_lbs_each to null. Do not invent bale, Gaylord, pallet, or roll weights merely to satisfy structure.
- correction agent_1_value and final_value may be strings or arrays. Preserve the evidence shape.
- Use supply_mode values one_time, ongoing, seasonal, or unclear.
- Return the schema object only. Do not add a second output wrapper yourself; the n8n agent runtime owns its external output envelope.

reasons: Seller-stated quantity (120,000 lbs) and per load shipment (20,000-30,000 lbs/load) are confirmed at commercial scale and match a business seller from Charlotte, NC.
Material is described as post-industrial 'trim scrap from a printer', inferred to be BOPP film/parts from webform and context.

Direct visual audits confirm two distinct inventory types: (A) Loose, flat, printed flexible sheets in Gaylords (likely BOPP or release liner, though polymer visually uncertain) and (B) Die-cut rigid/contoured parts with printed labels in Gaylords, both with mixed printed colors (white dominant) and high print/label/adhesive presence.
Visible contamination is intrinsic (labels, adhesive, inks) and not gross debris or foreign matter. All observed units are stored indoors; no moisture or severe soiling observed.
Quality risks: High ink/adhesive/paper label contamination risk is observed but operational at scale for plastics recycling, meeting routeable commercial quality.

PDF SDS (BOPP film and OPP film) confirm the plausible source polymer family (polypropylene film) and standard recycling/commercial use context, supporting BOPP but do not override the presence of print/labels seen visually.

Direct unit counts confirm at least two unique Gaylord units with >2/3 full each, but seller states much larger ongoing volumes—site inventory not fully visible, but supply cadence and commercial volume is assured by form.
Material is not residential, not post-consumer, not drop-off, not a disallowed or non-plastic product.
Both agent 1 and agent 2 deduplicate visible units, and there is no evidence of intentional error or inventory inflation.
clarification_requests: Can you confirm whether all material is in the form of loose flexible film (as in photo 1) or if some is in rigid die-cut parts with attached labels (as in photo 2)? Are these two separate material streams, or do both comprise a single load?
Are all loads comprised primarily of BOPP as described, or is any other plastic or paper involved?
If possible, can you provide a photo of a complete shipment ready for pickup or loading, showing the typical quantity per shipment?

fields.Reason:
[
    "Seller-stated quantity (120,000 lbs) and per load shipment (20,000-30,000 lbs/load) are confirmed at commercial scale and match a business seller from Charlotte, NC.",
    "Material is described as post-industrial 'trim scrap from a printer', inferred to be BOPP film/parts from webform and context.",
    "Direct visual audits confirm two distinct inventory types: (A) Loose, flat, printed flexible sheets in Gaylords (likely BOPP or release liner, though polymer visually uncertain) and (B) Die-cut rigid/contoured parts with printed labels in Gaylords, both with mixed printed colors (white dominant) and high print/label/adhesive presence.",
    "Visible contamination is intrinsic (labels, adhesive, inks) and not gross debris or foreign matter. All observed units are stored indoors; no moisture or severe soiling observed.",
    "Quality risks: High ink/adhesive/paper label contamination risk is observed but operational at scale for plastics recycling, meeting routeable commercial quality.",
    "PDF SDS (BOPP film and OPP film) confirm the plausible source polymer family (polypropylene film) and standard recycling/commercial use context, supporting BOPP but do not override the presence of print/labels seen visually.",
    "Direct unit counts confirm at least two unique Gaylord units with >2/3 full each, but seller states much larger ongoing volumes—site inventory not fully visible, but supply cadence and commercial volume is assured by form.",
    "Material is not residential, not post-consumer, not drop-off, not a disallowed or non-plastic product.",
    "Both agent 1 and agent 2 deduplicate visible units, and there is no evidence of intentional error or inventory inflation."
]

fields.Clarification Requests:
[
    "Can you confirm whether all material is in the form of loose flexible film (as in photo 1) or if some is in rigid die-cut parts with attached labels (as in photo 2)? Are these two separate material streams, or do both comprise a single load?",
    "Are all loads comprised primarily of BOPP as described, or is any other plastic or paper involved?",
    "If possible, can you provide a photo of a complete shipment ready for pickup or loading, showing the typical quantity per shipment?",
    "Confirm whether the inventory is one-time or recurring and provide recurring cadence if applicable."
]
fields.Name: Igor Beylin
fields.Reason:
[
    "Seller-stated quantity (120,000 lbs) and per load shipment (20,000-30,000 lbs/load) are confirmed at commercial scale and match a business seller from Charlotte, NC.",
    "Material is described as post-industrial 'trim scrap from a printer', inferred to be BOPP film/parts from webform and context.",
    "Direct visual audits confirm two distinct inventory types: (A) Loose, flat, printed flexible sheets in Gaylords (likely BOPP or release liner, though polymer visually uncertain) and (B) Die-cut rigid/contoured parts with printed labels in Gaylords, both with mixed printed colors (white dominant) and high print/label/adhesive presence.",
    "Visible contamination is intrinsic (labels, adhesive, inks) and not gross debris or foreign matter. All observed units are stored indoors; no moisture or severe soiling observed.",
    "Quality risks: High ink/adhesive/paper label contamination risk is observed but operational at scale for plastics recycling, meeting routeable commercial quality.",
    "PDF SDS (BOPP film and OPP film) confirm the plausible source polymer family (polypropylene film) and standard recycling/commercial use context, supporting BOPP but do not override the presence of print/labels seen visually.",
    "Direct unit counts confirm at least two unique Gaylord units with >2/3 full each, but seller states much larger ongoing volumes—site inventory not fully visible, but supply cadence and commercial volume is assured by form.",
    "Material is not residential, not post-consumer, not drop-off, not a disallowed or non-plastic product.",
    "Both agent 1 and agent 2 deduplicate visible units, and there is no evidence of intentional error or inventory inflation."
]
fields.Notes: Trim scrap from printer; predominantly BOPP per seller; loose printed sheets with high ink/adhesive/label content and die-cut rigid plastic parts both present. Photos and paperwork support business, not residential, post-industrial supply. Indoor storage, no gross debris. Load weight normalized: 20000-30000 lb from "WeightPerLoad: 20000-30000". Current inventory evidence: 2 Gaylords. Recurring cadence: blank because no defensible weekly or monthly cadence was established. Estimated inventory weight (not for settlement): not calculable from defensible visible gross-weight evidence. Weight assumptions: Photos are representative of current or typical supply being offered.; Seller provides accurate commercial representation of recurring load size.. Weight limitations: Unable to visually estimate gross weight per Gaylord due to no visible scale, standardized weight, or packing density.; Total site inventory and shipment cadence not visible; seller statement only.; No defensible per-unit gross weight range was available; arithmetic was not fabricated.. Visible quality risks: Paper Labels/Adhesive [Observed/High]; Printing and Ink [Observed/Moderate].

workflowData.name: Web Lead Triage - PRODUCTION - Attachments v5.5.2

nodes.name: Create folder
nodes.notes: Creates the Drive folder after the mandatory Odoo attempt. All naming values are read directly from the immutable Webhook payload, not from the Odoo response item.

nodes.name: Normalize & Split Attachments
nodes.notes: v5.2 canonical attachment contract. Validates, deduplicates, stamps revision, and emits one item per Cognito file.

nodes.name: Upload Current Attachment
nodes.notes: v5.2 storage branch. Uploads the exact current binary with its authoritative Cognito filename.

nodes.name: Download Current Attachment
nodes.notes: Downloads exactly the current canonical Cognito attachment into binary.data.

nodes.name: Restore Original Filename
nodes.notes: v5.2 validates binary size and restores Cognito filename/MIME as the authoritative binary metadata.

parameters.text:
You are Agent 1, the Visual Material Inspector for Scrap Management Inc.
Inspect only the current original image. Emit a traceable Visual Material Evidence Packet. Separate visible facts, visual assessments, operational assumptions, and unknowns. Do not make the final HOT/COLD decision. Do not infer recurring cadence from a photograph. Do not claim exact polymer or exact weight without evidence.

Critical invariants:
- Material form is independent from containment and support unit.
- Loose film in Gaylords => material_form_observed "Loose", containment "Gaylords".
- Film rolls on pallets => material_form_observed "Film Rolls", support_unit "Pallets".
- Baled film => material_form_observed "Film Bales", containment "Bales".
- Never infer material form solely from packaging.
- Mixed is a first-class commercial color. Printed multicolor recyclable material is Mixed.
- Ignore pallet, container, floor, equipment, wall, and background colors when classifying material color.
- Outdoor-stored baled film creates Moisture risk with status "Operationally Assumed" and severity "High". Do not invent a moisture percentage.
- Count visible inventory units and flag possible duplicate views, but do not calculate total lot weight.
- Record roll-core observations only when visible. Use ranges for dimensions.

Canonical Process Type values:
Injection Molding; Blow Molding; Extrusion - Film; Extrusion - Sheet; Extrusion - Pipe/Profile; Thermoforming; Compression Molding; Rotational Molding; Compounding/Pelletizing; Foam Processing; Other; Unclear.

Canonical Material Type values:
Post Industrial; Post Commercial; Post Consumer; Virgin; Repro; Unclear.

Return raw JSON only with this exact top-level structure:

  "packet_version": "visual-material-evidence/v1",
  "material": {
    "material_family_visual": "string or Unclear",
    "material_form_observed": "Pellets | Regrind | Flake | Film Bales | Film Rolls | Logs/Agglo | Purge | Parts | Rigid Bales | Loose | Floorsweep | Sheet | Pipe/Profile | Foam | Other | Unclear",

nodes.name: Agent 1 - Visual Material Inspector

options.systemMessage:
# SYSTEM - Agent 2 Multimodal Lead Adjudicator V5.5

You are Agent 2 for Scrap Management Inc. Your direct-image phase has independently inspected every authoritative original image immediately upstream. You now reconcile those image audits with the complete Cognito webform, every Agent 1 Visual Material Evidence Packet, and every PDF Material Evidence Report.

Return supported business conclusions and explicitly preserve conflicts, evidence, assumptions, limitations, and unknowns. Never silently copy Agent 1. Correct it when the direct image audits disagree.

SOURCE AUTHORITY BY FIELD
1. Seller form: seller-stated quantity, cadence language, company, location, stated source, and stated contamination.
2. Current original images: current material form, containment, support unit, visible color, visible contamination, storage condition, and visible inventory units.
3. PDFs: polymer, composition, specifications, technical properties, explicit weights, and explicit process statements.
4. Direct Agent 2 audits outrank Agent 1 for visual facts.
5. A failed image or PDF analysis is not contrary evidence. Preserve it for manual review.

NORMALIZATION INVARIANTS
- Process Type values: Injection Molding; Blow Molding; Extrusion - Film; Extrusion - Sheet; Extrusion - Pipe/Profile; Thermoforming; Compression Molding; Rotational Molding; Compounding/Pelletizing; Foam Processing; Other; Unclear.
- Material Type values: Post Industrial; Post Commercial; Post Consumer; Virgin; Repro; Unclear.
- Form is the recyclable material form. Packaging is containment/support for shipment.
- Loose film in Gaylords => material_form Loose, packaging Gaylords.
- Film rolls on pallets => material_form Film Rolls, packaging Pallets.
- Baled film => material_form Film Bales, packaging Bales.
- Never infer form solely from packaging.
- Mixed is a valid commercial color. Printed multicolor recyclable material is Mixed. Ignore pallet, container, floor, equipment, and background colors.
- Do not infer polymer solely from appearance.

QUANTITY AND CADENCE
- Distinguish current inventory, pounds per load, and recurring shipment cadence.
- "3 loads" is a current load count unless time-basis language establishes cadence. It is never 3 pounds.
- Extract explicit weekly, monthly, every-other-week, twice-monthly, quarterly, annual, and range language.
- Do not perform weekly/monthly conversion arithmetic. Deterministic code owns conversion.
- Unknown cadence is null, not zero. Use zero only when the seller explicitly states zero.
- Ongoing but unspecified supply has null cadence and a clarification request.

WEIGHT ESTIMATION
- Deduplicate visible units across images using candidate duplicate groups, spatial identity, labels, damage marks, roll/core geometry, and scene overlap.
- Emit unique units and per-unit gross weight ranges only when supportable.
- Do not deduct tare or total the lot. Deterministic code owns arithmetic.
- Mark whether the estimate covers the complete inventory or visible inventory only.
- Outdoor/moisture conditions widen uncertainty. Do not estimate exact water content.

QUALITY RISKS
- Outdoor-stored baled film => Moisture, High, Operationally Assumed.
- Preserve commercial routing impacts without inventing measurements.

QUALIFICATION
Preserve the existing thresholds:
- Hot: confirmed >=10,000 lb or equivalent full truckload, plastic, business/commercial/industrial source.
- Cold: explicit residential/individual, <8,000 lb, drop-off, non-plastic, or disallowed material.
- Unclear Hot: potentially qualified but missing critical confirmation. Unclear Hot routes to HOT.

Return raw structured JSON only through the attached parser. Every top-level key must exist.

OUTPUT CONTRACT HARD RULES
- decision must be exactly one of: Hot, Unclear Hot, Cold.
- airtable_target must be exactly HOT or COLD. Never return a table name, lead label, routing description, or offer ID in this field.
- email_template must be exactly one template identifier: Qualified A, Rejection B, Rejection C, Rejection D, or Rejection E. Never draft the email body in this field.
- Nullable numeric fields are genuinely nullable. When no defensible gross unit weight exists, set gross_weight_low_lbs_each and gross_weight_high_lbs_each to null. Do not invent bale, Gaylord, pallet, or roll weights merely to satisfy structure.
- correction agent_1_value and final_value may be strings or arrays. Preserve the evidence shape.
- Use supply_mode values one_time, ongoing, seasonal, or unclear.
- Return the schema object only. Do not add a second output wrapper yourself; the n8n agent runtime owns its external output envelope.

nodes.name: Agent 2 - Final Reconciliation
nodes.notes: V5.5.2 Agent 2 reconciliation with nullable evidence schema and exact decision/target/template contracts.

nodes.name: Agent 2 Structured Output Parser
nodes.notes: V5.5.2 manual JSON Schema. Nullable numeric fields use number|null, correction values may be strings or arrays, and unsupported visual weights remain null without parser failure.

nodes.name: POST Raw Cognito to Production Odoo
nodes.notes: V5.5.2 Odoo-first POST using the named Odoo API bearer credential. Returns the parsed JSON body only (fullResponse=false), preventing IncomingMessage/TLSSocket/ClientRequest circular graphs from entering execution data. HTTP responses never hard-stop this branch; the allowlisted normalizer and final gate decide success. Automatic POST retries are disabled until server-side idempotency by Cognito Entry.Number is verified.

nodes.name: Build Attachment Result
nodes.notes: v5.2 produces one complete attachment result inside the one-item loop and returns it to Loop Over Attachments.

nodes.name: Finalize Attachment Batch
nodes.notes: V5.5.2 validates the locked attachment loop and emits the complete manifest, Agent 1 reports, PDF reports, native Airtable attachments, and source identities needed for Agent 2 direct-image review.

nodes.name: Is PDF?
nodes.notes: Routes non-image attachments by canonical MIME type. True=PDF; false=unsupported.

nodes.name: Normalize Drive Upload
nodes.notes: Validates Drive identity, filename, MIME type, and size; emits storage result keyed by Cognito sourceFileId.

nodes.name: Normalize Image Analysis
nodes.notes: Normalizes OpenAI binary vision output into the v5.2 contract within the current attachment loop.

nodes.name: Normalize Unsupported Analysis
nodes.notes: Emits one v5.2 unsupported analysis result for the current attachment without blocking storage.

nodes.name: Loop Over Attachments
nodes.notes: v5.2 deterministic per-attachment loop. Each iteration must return exactly one complete attachment result before the next item begins.

nodes.name: Extract PDF Text
nodes.notes: v5.2 extracts searchable PDF text locally from binary.data before LLM analysis. This removes the failing Gemini Files API dependency for ordinary text PDFs.

nodes.name: PDF Text Available?
nodes.notes: Routes text-bearing PDFs to OpenAI analysis and unreadable/scanned PDFs to an explicit failed analysis contract.

nodes.name: Final Production Gate

nodes.name: Deterministic Evidence & Calculation Gate

parameters.text:
=You are Agent 2, the Multimodal Lead Adjudicator for Scrap Management Inc. This is your direct visual audit of one authoritative original image.

You MUST inspect the binary image yourself. Audit Agent 1 rather than merely summarizing it. The full webform and all attachment reports are provided so you can identify conflicts, correct field separation, and preserve source authority.

Rules:
- Current image evidence controls current material form, containment/support, visible color, visible contamination, unit identity, and storage condition.
- PDFs may control polymer/composition/technical properties when specifically linked, but cannot override the current image for packaging, form, visible color, contamination, or storage.
- Form and logistics are separate. Gaylords do not make the material form Gaylords. Pallets do not make the form Pallet Stacks.
- Printed multicolor recyclable material is commercial color Mixed. Ignore pallet, container, floor, machinery, and background colors.
- Inspect process clues and lifecycle/source clues, but label inferences and confidence.
- Identify visible units and candidate duplicate groups so the final reconciliation can deduplicate repeated views.
- Do not calculate final inventory weight. Provide per-unit gross ranges only when visually supportable; deterministic code will total and deduct tare.
- Outdoor-stored baled film => Moisture, High, Operationally Assumed. Do not invent a moisture percentage.
- Do not infer weekly/monthly cadence from images.

CURRENT IMAGE METADATA AND AGENT 1 PACKET:
{{ $json.toJsonString() }}

COMPLETE COGNITO WEBFORM:
{{ $('Webhook').first().json.body.toJsonString() }}

ALL AGENT 1 AND PDF REPORTS:
{{ $('Finalize Attachment Batch').first().json.attachmentAnalysis.toJsonString() }}

Return raw JSON only:

    "containment": "Gaylords | Supersacks | Bales | Crates | Drums | Bags | None | Other | Unclear",
    "support_unit": "Pallets | Racks | Floor Loaded | None | Other | Unclear",
    "packaging_for_airtable": "Bales | Supersacks | Gaylords | Pallets | Loose | Crates | Drums | Bags | Other | Unclear",
    "commercial_color": "Natural | Mixed | White | Black | Green | Blue | Red | Yellow | Gray | Custom | Unknown",
    "dominant_color": "string or Unknown",
    "secondary_colors": ["string"],
    "cleanliness": "Clean | Mixed | Dirty | Unclear",
    "visible_contaminants": ["string"],
    "outdoor_storage": "Observed | Likely | Not Observed | Not Assessable",
    "confidence_by_field": {"field_name": 0.0}
  },

    "agreement": "Full | Partial | Disagree | Unavailable",

  },

    {
      "candidate_unit_id": "string",
